# Literature Review

This chapter surveys the academic foundations relevant to reinforcement learning
for high-frequency trading. It is organized in two parts. The first part reviews
market microstructure — the theoretical mechanisms and empirical evidence that
govern short-horizon price formation in limit order book markets. This
establishes the financial environment in which the agent operates and motivates
the feature engineering choices made in Chapter 4. The second part positions
reinforcement learning as the appropriate framework for this problem. It then
surveys the key concepts that matter in a trading context and reviews prior RL
trading applications, with attention to action-space design, transaction-cost
modeling, and evaluation rigor. The survey concludes by identifying the
comparatively underexplored combination of LOB-derived features, continuous
control, and microstructure-aware state design that this thesis studies.
